value.body:
## Problem
The issue detail panel still gates initiative progress on the legacy `initiative` ticket type, but the current create/list flow has migrated initiatives to canonical `epic` tickets. As a result, an epic opened from the Initiatives page will not fetch or render its child progress.

## Evidence
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ViewIssuePanel.tsx:371` only calls `useInitiativeProgressQuery` when `issueQuery.data?.type === "initiative"`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/ViewIssuePanel.tsx:560` only renders the Progress property when `issue.type === "initiative"`.
- `packages/desktop/src/renderer/screens/WorkspaceScreen.tsx` now sets initiative-created tickets to `epic` and filters the Initiatives page with `{ type: "epic" }`.

## Expected
Canonical `epic` tickets should fetch and display progress, while legacy `initiative` tickets should continue to work during migration.

## Suggested fix
Normalize the issue type before checking it, or gate progress on `issue.type === "epic" || issue.type === "initiative"`. Add a renderer test covering an epic with child progress.

frontmatter.title: Epic tickets do not show initiative progress in issue details

value.title: Epic tickets do not show initiative progress in issue details
